title: serpyllopsis caespitosa var. dusenii
intro: Serpyllopsis caespitosa var. dusenii, also known as Brazilian rosemary, is a shrubby plant belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving cognitive function, and aiding in digestion. Brazilian rosemary can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
